SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain

Class ParallelSweep

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain.ParallelSweep
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ParallelSweep extends Object implements Serializable
Evaluates a batch of decision vectors in parallel, preserving input order.

The agentic optimizer evaluates candidates one at a time; for parameter sweeps, Latin-hypercube sampling or population-based search the trials are independent and can run concurrently. This utility maps a list of decision vectors over a thread pool and returns the results in the same order as the inputs, turning an expensive sequential sweep into a parallel one.

Each trial is scored by a caller-supplied ParallelSweep.SweepEvaluator. Because NeqSim flowsheet objects are not thread-safe, the evaluator must give every trial its own state — the canonical pattern is to call ProcessSystem.copy() (or rebuild the flowsheet) inside the evaluator so each worker thread owns an independent model. Evaluators that only read immutable inputs and return a fresh result object are safe as written.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

    ParallelSweep.SweepEvaluator<R>

    Description
    ParallelSweep()
    Creates a parallel sweep using a default thread count equal to the number of available processors.

  • Constructor Details

    • ParallelSweep

      public ParallelSweep()
      Creates a parallel sweep using a default thread count equal to the number of available processors.

  • Method Details

    • setParallelism

      public ParallelSweep setParallelism(int parallelism)
      Sets the number of worker threads.
      Parameters:
      parallelism - the number of worker threads (must be positive)
      Returns:
      this sweep for method chaining

    • getParallelism

      public int getParallelism()
      Gets the configured number of worker threads.
      Returns:
      the number of worker threads

    • run

      public <R> List<R> run(List<double[]> inputs, ParallelSweep.SweepEvaluator<R> evaluator)
      Evaluates all decision vectors in parallel and returns the results in input order.
      Type Parameters:
      R - the result type produced for each decision vector
      Parameters:
      inputs - the decision vectors to evaluate (must not be null)
      evaluator - the per-trial evaluator (must not be null and must be thread-safe)
      Returns:
      a list of results in the same order as inputs
